evals.prompt: I'm building a FastAPI app (Python) and I've never set up CI/CD before. I'm on GitHub. The app has pytest tests and uses PostgreSQL. What do I need to set up and how do I do it?
evals.expected_output: A complete GitHub Actions CI workflow config covering lint, test (with postgres service container), and a CD step to deploy on main. Should include secrets handling, caching, and explain the pipeline stages.
expectations: Provides a complete GitHub Actions YAML workflow file
Includes a PostgreSQL service container for integration tests
Covers both CI (on PR) and CD (on main merge) stages
Explains how to handle secrets via GitHub Actions secrets
Includes dependency caching to speed up runs
Mentions a deploy step (even if templated) for the CD stage

evals.prompt: We have a Node.js Express API that works locally. 3-person startup, pre-launch. We need to actually deploy this thing. Should we use Docker? What platform? We have almost no DevOps experience and a tight budget.
evals.expected_output: A concrete deployment recommendation (Fly.io or Railway for budget + simplicity), Dockerfile for the Node app, and steps to get it running. Should address health checks, secrets, and how to deploy.
expectations: Recommends a specific platform appropriate for the context (Fly.io or Railway are best fits)
Provides a complete Dockerfile for a Node.js app
Includes a health check endpoint recommendation
Explains how to handle environment variables / secrets
Gives concrete deployment commands or steps
Does not recommend Kubernetes (overkill for this context)

evals.prompt: My production FastAPI service has been running for 6 months and I realized I have zero monitoring — no alerts, no logs, nothing. Something broke last week and I only found out from a user complaint 4 hours later. What's the minimum I need to add right now to catch outages quickly?
evals.expected_output: A prioritized minimum viable observability setup: Sentry for errors, uptime monitoring for the /health endpoint, structured logging. Should give concrete code snippets and explain which to do first.
expectations: Recommends Sentry or equivalent for error tracking with a code snippet
Recommends an uptime/health check monitoring service (BetterUptime, Checkly, etc.)
Suggests adding a /health endpoint if not present
Recommends structured logging over print statements
Prioritizes what to do first vs. later
Keeps scope tight — this is 'minimum viable', not a full observability platform
